title: USDA Value Syntax Production Contract
format_neutral_rules: contracts/document-model-productions/attribute-spec.contract.json — the TimeSamples and Spline value-shape rules, default-vs-typeName agreement, and value-block sentinel semantics are pinned in the format-neutral production. This contract adds USDA-specific grammar productions (TimeSampleMap, SplineMap, AssetRef forms, PathRef forms, LayerOffset).

included: asset references as authored asset values
None as a value block where the consuming production permits it
time sample maps as authored attribute field values
spline maps as authored attribute field values
declared attribute type validation and allowed numeric coercion

requirements: single-at asset refs terminate at the next at sign and may not contain CR/LF
triple-at asset refs terminate only at the matching triple-at delimiter
asset refs are stored as authored asset values and are not resolved by this parser
path text between angle brackets must be delegated to usd-paths
empty path refs are represented as the empty path/reference value where the consuming field permits it

time_sample_map.storage: native TimeSamples field value keyed by numeric time code with typed values or value-block sentinels
requirements: time sample keys are numeric ordinates internally, not strings; string keys are canonical dump output only
time sample values must be parsed through the same TypedValue path as defaults
None inside a time sample stores a value-block sentinel
samples are stored as authored layer data and are not interpolated or resolved
sample value types should agree with the containing attribute typeName when the type is known

spline_map.storage: native Spline field value retaining curve type, extrapolation, loop, knot, tangent, interpolation, and custom-data authoring
requirements: duplicate curve type, extrapolation, loop, or knot subfields use last-one-wins as specified by the spline text format
post knot items carry segment interpolation mode, not a separate post tangent mode
spline data is stored for editing/value-resolution consumers but is not evaluated by the parser
spline records must not be represented internally as Python dict/list payloads, std::map<string, Json>, or canonical dump object trees
within one offset parameter list, repeated offset or scale fields use last-one-wins

laws: value parsing must consume usda-lexical-format for tokenization and string/comment handling
path references must consume usd-paths and must not use an independent path parser
foundational values must be represented through usd-foundational-values storage
authored listOp values must be represented through usd-listops-authored storage
dictionary values must retain typed entries in native storage and materialize JSON only for adapters
unknown declared attribute types have undefined validation behavior unless a golden requires a diagnostic
known declared foundational types must reject values that cannot be coerced to the declared type
None must be accepted only where the consuming production maps it to a value block or empty authored listOp
golden_groups: numeric coercion and type mismatch diagnostics
tuples, arrays, vectors, matrices, and semantic alias values
time sample maps with blocked samples
spline maps with repeated last-one-wins items
